req.title: Recurring transfer (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two equivalence partitions for tier-based limits (standard/premium), a hard boundary at $2,500 for standard and $10,000 for premium, an idempotency contract for duplicate submissions, and two explicit 422 error paths (limit exceeded, unsupported frequency). Tests are designed to cover: (1) EP – valid premium schedule within limit; (2) NEG – standard account exceeds $2,500 cap; (3) BVA – standard account at exactly $2,500 (upper boundary, valid); (4) DT – idempotency: duplicate key returns original result; (5) NEG – unsupported frequency yields 422. All tests are self-contained via fixtures and use a unique Idempotency-Key per run via uuid4.

tests.name: test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit
expected: Persisted schedule reflects amount=9000 and frequency=WEEKLY
trace: R-FIN-080: premium tier cap $10,000; $9,000 is within partition [0, 10000]
R-FIN-080: schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, assert_schedule_persisted

def test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit(<premium_customer_token>, <api_base_url>):
    client = api_client(<api_base_url>, token=<premium_customer_token>)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 9000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "source_account_id": <premium_source_account>,
        "destination_account_id": <destination_account>
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    resp = client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body
    assert "next_run" in body
    assert body["amount"] == 9000
    assert body["frequency"] == "WEEKLY"
    assert_schedule_persisted(client, body["schedule_id"], expected_amount=9000)

tests.name: test_standard_recurring_transfer_exceeds_cap_returns_422
expected: Response body contains error code indicating per-occurrence limit exceeded
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, schedule_exists

def test_standard_recurring_transfer_exceeds_cap_returns_422(<standard_customer_token>, <api_base_url>):
    client = api_client(<api_base_url>, token=<standard_customer_token>)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 2501,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "source_account_id": <standard_source_account>,
        "destination_account_id": <destination_account>
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    resp = client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "limit" in error_text or "exceed" in error_text or "cap" in error_text
    assert not schedule_exists(client, idempotency_key)

BVA: test on-point at maximum allowed value for standard tier

tests.name: test_idempotent_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result
preconditions: First POST with Idempotency-Key has already succeeded and returned 201
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with amount=5000, frequency=WEEKLY, same Idempotency-Key (first submission)
POST /transfers/recurring with identical payload and same Idempotency-Key (duplicate submission)
expected: First call returns HTTP 201 with schedule_id and next_run
Second call returns HTTP 200 or 201 with identical schedule_id
Exactly one schedule record exists (no duplicate created)
trace: R-FIN-080: operation must be idempotent; duplicate Idempotency-Key returns original result
DT: decision row: same key + same payload => replay cached response
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, count_schedules_for_key

def test_idempotent_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result(<premium_customer_token>, <api_base_url>):
    client = api_client(<api_base_url>, token=<premium_customer_token>)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "source_account_id": <premium_source_account>,
        "destination_account_id": <destination_account>
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    first = client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201
    first_body = first.json()
    second = client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code in (200, 201)
    second_body = second.json()
    assert second_body["schedule_id"] == first_body["schedule_id"]
    assert second_body["next_run"] == first_body["next_run"]
    assert count_schedules_for_key(client, idempotency_key) == 1

tests.name: test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422
preconditions: HOURLY is not a supported frequency value
trace: NEG: invalid frequency value outside allowed set
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_client, schedule_exists

def test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422(<premium_customer_token>, <api_base_url>):
    client = api_client(<api_base_url>, token=<premium_customer_token>)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 1000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "HOURLY",
        "source_account_id": <premium_source_account>,
        "destination_account_id": <destination_account>
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    resp = client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "frequency" in error_text or "unsupported" in error_text or "invalid" in error_text
    assert not schedule_exists(client, idempotency_key)

tests.name: test_premium_boundary_at_max_cap_10000
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with amount=10000 (exact premium cap), frequency=WEEKLY
trace: R-FIN-080: premium tier cap $10,000; $10,000 is on-point upper boundary
BVA: on-point at maximum allowed value for premium tier
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from <helper_fn> import api_client

def test_premium_boundary_at_max_cap_10000(<premium_customer_token>, <api_base_url>):
    client = api_client(<api_base_url>, token=<premium_customer_token>)
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "amount": 10000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "source_account_id": <premium_source_account>,
        "destination_account_id": <destination_account>
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    resp = client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["amount"] == 10000
    assert "schedule_id" in body
    next_run = datetime.fromisoformat(body["next_run"].replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    assert next_run > datetime.now(timezone.utc)
